title: Nirvana vs Things 3 for Power users
meta_description: Nirvana vs Things 3 for power users running strict GTD systems with granular context filtering and review workflows.
constraint_lens: You run a strict GTD workflow and need granular context filters and structured review depth.

verdict.summary: Nirvana wins for power users implementing a strict GTD system. It includes dedicated lists for Next, Waiting, and Scheduled, plus context-based filtering built into the model. Things 3 supports areas, projects, and tags but does not enforce deep GTD stages or review layers. If context-based filtering and review workflow depth are constrained, Things 3 fails first.
verdict.decision_rule: If context-based filtering and review workflow depth are constrained, Things 3 fails first.

sections.heading: Best fit for structured GTD execution
sections.content: You run strict GTD workflows and expect granular context filtering. Nirvana separates tasks into GTD stages such as Next and Waiting and allows filtering by context directly. Things 3 organizes work into areas and projects with tags, but does not center the system around GTD stages.

bullets.point: Dedicated GTD stage lists such as Next and Waiting
bullets.why_it_matters: You move tasks through defined stages instead of managing everything in general project lists.

bullets.point: Context-based filtering built into task views
bullets.why_it_matters: You filter tasks by location or tool without building custom perspectives.
You systematically review projects and next actions using the built-in workflow.
You organize responsibilities simply without managing GTD stage transitions.

bullets.point: Forecast view combining calendar and tasks
bullets.why_it_matters: You see upcoming tasks and events together without configuring complex filters.
You capture tasks rapidly without navigating multiple GTD-specific lists.

sections.heading: Where each tool can break down

items.fails_when: You want a lighter system without managing strict GTD stages and context lists.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Things 3 if you prefer a simpler project-based workflow.

items.fails_when: You require granular context filters and clearly separated GTD stages for review.
items.what_to_do_instead: Switch to Nirvana for deeper GTD alignment.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: If you follow a loose version of GTD and mainly rely on tags and simple project lists, Things 3 may feel sufficient.

sections.heading: Quick rules
rules: If you need built-in GTD stages and context filters, choose Nirvana.
If you prefer a clean project-focused system, consider Things 3.
If weekly review depth matters, avoid lighter task-only models.

faqs.q: Does Things 3 support strict GTD stages?
faqs.a: It supports projects and tags but does not enforce dedicated GTD stage lists.

faqs.q: Can Nirvana filter tasks by context?
faqs.a: Yes. Context-based filtering is built into its core views.

faqs.q: Which is better for advanced GTD workflows?
faqs.a: Nirvana is generally better for strict GTD implementation.

faqs.q: Which is simpler for general task tracking?
faqs.a: Things 3 is simpler if you do not need full GTD structure.
